name: Remote Receiver
description: The `remote_receiver` component lets you receive and decode various common remote control signals, such as infrared or 433 MHz radio frequency (RF) signals.

config_entries.label: Pin
config_entries.description: The pin to receive the remote signal on.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Pin mode flags (input / output / pullup / pulldown / open_drain). Combine flags as needed — e.g. input + pullup for a button pulled to VCC.

config_entries.label: Input
config_entries.description: Set the input mode flag.

config_entries.label: Output
config_entries.description: Set the output mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pullup
config_entries.description: Set the pullup mode flag.

config_entries.label: Pulldown
config_entries.description: Set the pulldown mode flag.

config_entries.label: Open Drain
config_entries.description: Set the open drain mode flag.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Invert the logical level. ``true`` swaps high/low in software so an active-low button reads as active when grounded.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID used for code generation. Useful when multiple receivers are configured on a single device.

config_entries.label: Buffer Size
config_entries.description: The size of the internal buffer for storing the remote codes. Defaults to `10kB` on the ESP32 and `1kB` on the ESP8266.

config_entries.label: Carrier Duty Percent
config_entries.description: The carrier duty cycle for signal demodulation in the RMT peripheral in Hz. Defaults to `100`.

config_entries.label: Carrier Frequency
config_entries.description: The carrier frequency for signal demodulation in the RMT peripheral in Hz. Defaults to `0Hz` (carrier demodulation disabled).

config_entries.label: Clock Resolution
config_entries.description: The clock resolution used by the RMT peripheral in Hz. Defaults to `1000000`.

config_entries.label: Dump
config_entries.description: Decode and dump these remote codes in the logs (at log.level=DEBUG). Set to `all` to dump all available codecs:

config_entries.label: Filter
config_entries.description: Filter any pulses that are shorter than this. Useful for removing glitches from noisy signals. Allowed values are in range `0` to `4294967295us`. Defaults to `50us`.

config_entries.label: Filter Symbols
config_entries.description: Filter out any data received with a length in symbols less than `filter_symbols`. Useful for filtering out short bursts of noise.

config_entries.label: Idle
config_entries.description: The amount of time that a signal should remain stable/unchanged for it to be considered complete. Defaults to `10ms`. The maximum allowable value is:

config_entries.label: Receive Symbols
config_entries.description: Maximum receive length in symbols. On some variants the maximum receive is limited to `rmt_symbols`.

config_entries.label: RMT Symbols
config_entries.description: When `use_dma` is enabled, this sets the size of the driver's internal DMA buffer. When DMA is disabled, it specifies how much RMT memory is allocated to the component. RMT memory is shared across all components and should be allocated in multiples of the block size. On the `ESP32` and `ESP32-S2` variants, RMT memory is shared between RX and TX components. On other variants, RX and TX have dedicated RMT memory.

config_entries.label: Tolerance
config_entries.description: The percentage or time that the remote signal lengths can deviate in the decoding process. Defaults to `25%`.

config_entries.label: Use DMA
config_entries.description: Enable DMA on variants that support it. If enabled `rmt_symbols` controls the DMA buffer size and can be set to a large value.
